commit.message:
fix: include Stadium Agoura_* amps in ToneIntent schema enum

AMP_NAMES only included standard (HD2_*) amp model names, causing Zod
validation to reject Agoura_* amps that the planner correctly selected
for Stadium presets. On retry, Claude fell back to standard amp names
which passed validation but produced empty presets (Stadium hardware
doesn't recognize HD2_* model IDs).

Now AMP_NAMES combines both AMP_MODELS and STADIUM_AMPS keys. The
planner prompt already filters which amps Claude sees per device.

Fixes both: "invalid_value on ampName" and "preset is empty" for Stadium.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add sales_manager to VALID_ROLES in admin users API

Was missing from the server-side allowlist, causing role updates to
return 400 Invalid role and revert in the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui-server): HMR fast refresh fixes and dev server debugging tools (#894)

* fix(ui-server): add HMR boundary wrapper for fast-refresh runtime

The dist build of fast-refresh-runtime.js has its import.meta.hot.accept()
DCE'd by bunup (import.meta.hot is undefined at library build time). This
caused Bun to cascade HMR updates through @vertz/ui dist chunks all the way
to the HTML entry point, triggering full page reloads on every file change.

Fix: generate a thin fast-refresh-init.js wrapper at .vertz/dev/ that imports
the runtime and calls import.meta.hot.accept(), creating an HMR boundary that
absorbs framework module updates and prevents full page reloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-compiler): preserve inline whitespace in JSX text between expressions

Replace .trim() with proper JSX whitespace handling in jsx-transformer.ts.
Text like "AHOY {name}" was losing the space between text and expressions.

- Add cleanJsxText() that preserves single-line spaces, collapses multi-line
- Use getFullText() instead of getText() to include leading trivia
- Add configurable editor for error overlay links (vscode, cursor, zed, webstorm)
  via VERTZ_EDITOR env var or editor option

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): parse SSR error stacks for file/line in overlay

SSR errors were broadcast with only the message, no file/line/lineText.
Now parseSourceFromStack() extracts source location from the error stack,
giving the overlay clickable editor links and code snippets for SSR errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): use .ts wrapper for fast-refresh runtime and hide Bun overlay

- Generate fast-refresh-init.ts (not .js) so Bun's dev bundler processes it
  and resolves bare specifiers like @vertz/ui/internals
- Hide Bun's <bun-hmr> error overlay via CSS since Vertz has its own
- Parse SSR error stacks for file/line/lineText in overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-compiler): unwrap NonNullExpression in reactivity analyzer [#890]

The `!` (non-null assertion) operator on useContext() calls caused the
reactivity analyzer to misclassify the variable as static instead of
a reactive source. This happened because the analyzer checked for
`CallExpression` directly, but `useContext(Ctx)!` wraps the call in
a `NonNullExpression` node.

The fix unwraps `NonNullExpression` before checking for `CallExpression`,
so `const settings = useContext(SettingsCtx)!` is correctly classified
as a reactive source — enabling proper `__conditional` rendering and
JSX transformation inside ternary branches.

Without this fix, JSX inside ternary branches that depend on the context
value remained as raw JSX in the compiled output, causing SSR failures
(Bun's native JSX compiler processed the remnants with the wrong runtime).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(task-manager): replace useContext(...)! with useSettings() [#890]

Replace the NonNullExpression pattern with the existing useSettings()
convenience function that throws a clear error when called outside
a Provider.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): use .ts wrapper for SSR module reload to preserve plugin processing [#890]

Bun's plugin system matches the `onLoad` filter against the full module
specifier. Appending `?t=...` cache-bust query to a `.tsx` entry path
caused the filter `/\.tsx$/` to NOT match, so Bun loaded the entry with
native JSX instead of the Vertz compiler. Native JSX evaluates children
eagerly (no thunks), breaking the synchronous context stack used by
Provider/useContext — causing `useContext()` to return undefined after
SSR module reload.

Fix: import through a thin `.ts` wrapper that re-exports from the real
entry. The wrapper gets `?t=...` (it's `.ts`, no plugin needed), while
the `.tsx` entry is imported with a clean path that matches the plugin
filter.

Also removes the now-unnecessary `examples/task-manager/index.html`
(dev server generates HTML shell dynamically via hmr-shell.html).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui-server): add dev server debugging tools and architecture docs [#890]

Add diagnostic infrastructure to reduce debugging time for dev server issues:

- ARCHITECTURE.md: comprehensive doc covering system overview, request flow,
  plugin pipeline, SSR render, file watcher, native vs compiled JSX, context
  system, and key footguns
- .claude/rules/dev-server-debugging.md: troubleshooting guide auto-loaded in
  Claude sessions with patterns for context undefined, reload loops, stale SSR,
  CSS disappearing, and build check false positives
- VERTZ_DEBUG env var: opt-in NDJSON logging to .vertz/dev/debug.log with
  category filtering (plugin, ssr, watcher, ws)
- /__vertz_diagnostics endpoint: JSON snapshot of server state (plugin config,
  processed files, SSR reload status, HMR assets, errors, WS clients)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(task-manager): fix icon sizing and apply formatting [#890]

Fix lucide-static SVG icons not respecting the size prop — the old code
prepended width/height attributes but HTML last-attribute-wins meant the
original 24px always took precedence. Now replaces existing attributes.
Also reduce icon size from 16 to 14 for better button proportions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add changeset for ui-compiler and ui-server [#890]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(task-manager): restore index.html removed during rebase [#890]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feat: Integrate CAD Viewer into ProjectDetailPage

**Added Components:**
- Viewer3D.tsx: Full 3D model viewer with toolbar controls
- ViewerToolbar.tsx: Controls for solid/wireframe, grid, measurement, cut plane, fullscreen

- CutPlane.tsx & CutPlaneControls.tsx: Cross-section visualization
- ObjectTree.tsx: Scene object hierarchy navigation
- MeasurementLine.tsx & MeasurementReadout.tsx: Point-to-point measurement tools

- useGLTFLoader.ts: Model loading and scene tree management
- ThemeContext.tsx: Dark/light theme support

**Updated:**
- App.tsx: Wrapped with ThemeProvider
- ProjectDetailPage.tsx: Replaced CAD placeholder with Viewer3D component

**Features:**
- Solid and wireframe rendering modes
- Grid display with toggle
- Measurement tool (click two points to measure distance)
- Cross-section/cut plane visualization
- Object tree for scene navigation
- Fullscreen mode (F key)
- Keyboard shortcuts: F/S/W/G/M/C/Esc

- Dark mode support

Version view coming in Phase 5.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Z.ai concurrency guard and migrate provider URL

Adds a Durable Object-backed concurrency limiter for z-ai-pro routes to
enforce Z.ai published per-model in-flight limits. Requests exceeding
the limit receive a 429 before hitting upstream. Failures fail open so
a DO outage never blocks production traffic.

Also migrates the Z.ai base URL from bigmodel.cn to api.z.ai (verified
live), and fixes the credit-exhaustion retry path to release the
concurrency lease before falling back to OpenRouter.

Changes:
- New ProviderConcurrency DO with lease-based tracking and TTL expiry
- Service layer enforces limits only for z-ai-pro; all others pass through
- Concurrency acquire/release wired into legacy chat request path with
  correct stream lifecycle handling and idempotent release
- Release lease before credit-exhaustion retry to avoid holding z-ai-pro
  slot during OpenRouter fallback
- wrangler.toml: add PROVIDER_CONCURRENCY binding and v3 migration using
  new_classes (not new_sqlite_classes, DO is in-memory only)
- Updated Z.ai URL in router, docs, and tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(statusline): unify cache TTLs into 3 tiers

Replace 4 separate TTL constants (PR_CACHE_TTL, CI_CACHE_TTL,
LIMITS_CACHE_TTL, SLOT_CACHE_TTL) with 3 semantic tiers:

- API_CACHE_TTL (120s) — anything that hits an API (CI, PR, limits)
- SLOT_CACHE_TTL (60s) — external commands (overridable in config)
- GH_CHECK_TTL (1800s) — gh CLI availability check (rarely changes)

PR was 300s, CI was 120s — no real reason for the difference since
both are GitHub API data. Now unified at 120s.

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): ensure query_end fires when generator is abandoned early (#6326)

## Summary
- When `.show()` collects enough rows, it breaks out of the `run_iter`
generator early. Python sends `GeneratorExit` (a `BaseException`) which
none of the existing exception handlers caught, so `_notify_query_end`
was never called and the dashboard stayed stuck in "Finalizing" forever.
- Handle `GeneratorExit` in `run_iter` to notify subscribers before
returning.
- Add regression test that verifies `on_query_end` fires for both
`.collect()` and `.show()`.

Closes DF-1664

## Test plan
- [x] New test `test_show_fires_query_end` reproduces the bug (fails
without the fix, passes with it)
- [x] All existing subscriber tests pass (`tests/test_subscribers.py`)
- [x] Manually verified with the dashboard — query transitions from
Finalizing to Finished

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: vol_shadow 16ms→0ms, shade 3.1ms→2.2ms — GPU total 22ms→5ms

vol_shadow pass:
- Reduce volume dimensions 256×128×256 → 64×32×64 (64× fewer texels)
- Reduce march steps 96→32, step size 0.15→0.5 (3× less per-texel work)
- Add coarse field early-out in shader (skip empty-space texels)
- Skip dispatch entirely on static frames (camera/sun unchanged)

shade pass:
- Reduce shadow steps 64→32, max distance 50→25m
- Reduce GI cone steps 48→24
- Reduce AO samples 6→4, step size 0.08→0.12

commit.message:
Recency-weighted candle scoring + Bullish Engulfing boost

analyzePriceAction.js — getPriceActionVerdict():
  Previously: only the single most-recent pattern contributed to paRaw.
  Now: all detected patterns within the last 10 candles are scored with
  recency weighting applied before summing:
    - Candles 1-5 (most recent, index >= n-5): 2× multiplier
    - Candles 6-10: 1× multiplier
  Contribution clamped to [-20, +30] before adding to paRaw.

  Point values (per occurrence × weight):
    Bullish Engulfing (high confidence): +15  (was +5 for any high bullish)
    Other bullish (high confidence):     + 5
    Other bullish (medium):              + 3
    Bearish (high confidence):           − 5
    Bearish (medium):                    − 2

ChartHeader.jsx — live trend context badge:
  Replaced hardcoded "Stage 2" pill with trendContext.humanReadableLabel
  and trendContext.color from the ['price-action', ticker] React Query cache.
  Falls back to "Stage 2" green while the cache is empty.

NVDA test (2026-03-04):
  60 candles, close $183.04, 13.7% below 52w high ($212.19)
  Bullish Engulfing (high, recent 2×) → patternRaw=30 clamped to 30
  priceActionPoints=41, score=66, verdict=watch, state=suppressed
  (caution + 3 distribution days triggers gate — correct for current setup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix WebSocket connection failures in archived builds

- yakker.sh: Replace fragile system Python detection with uv, a
  self-contained binary manager that downloads Python 3.12 automatically.
  Archived app subprocesses don't get a login shell, so PATH-based Python
  detection was picking up Xcode's Python 3.9 instead of a modern version,
  causing the WebSocket connection to be immediately closed by the server.
  uv is downloaded directly from GitHub releases as a tarball (no install
  script) so it works reliably from within an app bundle subprocess.

- yakker_stream.py: Make _extract_auth_value() strip repeated
  "Authorization:" prefixes to handle double-prefixed stored credentials.

- YakkerStreamManager.swift: Strip any "Authorization:" prefix from the
  stored auth key before constructing the header, preventing duplicate
  header values in edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove blocking 100ms delays from SCSI command handlers

The MsDelay(100) calls added in f86a166 block in IRQ context during
UNIT_ATTENTION handling, causing USB 2.0 hosts to timeout or miss
transfers after disc swaps. The SCSI protocol handles UNIT_ATTENTION
flow without artificial delays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bot goal reset loop: only trigger reached-goal logic when all path waypoints exhausted

Previously, `!stillMoving` fired when the bot reached any intermediate A* waypoint
(e.g. 1 block away), incorrectly triggering the reached-goal handler 28 blocks from
the actual target. This caused findMineableNearby() to find nothing and reset
currentGoal to ZERO every cycle, preventing the bot from ever moving.

Fix: gate the reached-goal block on `currentPath.isEmpty() || pathIndex >= currentPath.size()`
so it only triggers after all waypoints have been traversed.

commit.message:
trailing lambda: indentation-bounded multi-line lambdas without parens

When backslash appears as the last argument in a function application, the
parser bounds the lambda body by indentation (bodyCol - 1) so it
terminates when the next token drops below the body start column.
Enables Decoder \json -> ... and spawn \_ -> ... without wrapping
parens. One-line lambdas keep parens per convention.

Phase 4: ProjectDetailPage Redesign with Tree View & Purchased Parts Library

## Features Implemented
- **ProjectDetailPage**: Complete redesign with hierarchical tree view of parts
  - Visual tree with expandable/collapsible sub-assemblies
  - Part cards showing ID, name, type badge, and child count
  - Selected part detail panel (info, CAD placeholder, BOM, revisions)

- **Part Hierarchy**: Added parent_part_id field to support sub-assemblies
  - Parts can be nested under parent sub-assemblies
  - Tree structure built from flat parts list with proper indentation

- **Add Part Modal**: Enhanced with catalog part linking
  - For purchased parts: select from existing catalog or create new
  - Auto-populate part details from catalog selection
  - Optional parent sub-assembly selection

- **ProjectsPage**: Full CRUD for projects
  - List all projects with status

- **Purchased Parts Library**: Repurposed CatalogPage as central repository

- **Sidebar Navigation**: Improved with collapse/expand

- **Database Migration**: Added support for part hierarchies
